task: I’ve got a short message to send about a maintenance issue, and I want it to get to the point without sounding sloppy. Can you help me draft it, and keep it tight?

prefs.explanation: He values blunt honesty and dislikes sentimentality, so he is unlikely to want careful cushioning or euphemisms. Straight answers fit his transaction-first mindset better than polite buildup. If the help feels evasive, he will probably discount it quickly.

His style is practical and efficiency-minded, so long-winded explanations would likely feel like waste. He seems to want the useful part fast, with minimal ceremony. Too much text risks reading like someone trying to sell him something.

He would likely respond well to checklists, bullets, and other formats that make action and comparison easy. His comfort with paperwork and receipts suggests he appreciates organized presentation when it serves a task. Freeform, chatty responses are more likely to feel sloppy than helpful.

He does not seem to want heavy validation or emotionally warm framing. A brief acknowledgment of the situation is probably enough before moving to the practical answer. Overly supportive language would likely strike him as unnecessary decoration.

prefs.short: Prefers to stay in control of decisions
prefs.explanation: He tends to manage problems himself and dislikes relying on other people’s promises. In interaction, that suggests he would rather get options and consequences than be guided too aggressively. Prescriptive help is only welcome if it stays clearly under his control.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — paste the issue and what you want done, and I’ll tighten the message.

If you want a quick structure:
- what’s wrong
- what you want
- by when
- ask for confirmation in writing

Example:
> The maintenance issue at [location] still needs attention. Please confirm how you plan to fix it and when you can complete the work. I’d like a written update by [date].

If you paste your draft, I’ll make it shorter and cleaner.

per_pref.reason: Direct and blunt: "paste the issue and what you want done" and the example gets straight to the ask without softening.
Very concise and efficient: "keep it tight," a short structure, and "I’ll make it shorter and cleaner" all match the preference for no fluff.
Uses a clean functional format with bullets: "what’s wrong / what you want / by when / ask for confirmation in writing," which is exactly organized and actionable.
Minimal emotional framing; it acknowledges the task and moves straight to practical help. No heavy validation or comfort talk.
Supports user control by offering options and a structure rather than prescribing a single message: "If you want a quick structure" and "If you paste your draft" keep the user in charge.

turns.agent_response: The furnace is rattling again, and the hallway radiator still isn’t heating properly. Please check and repair both this week, and confirm the schedule in writing.

per_pref.reason: Very blunt and direct: "The furnace is rattling again... Please check and repair both this week" with no cushioning or euphemisms.
Extremely concise; it gives only the needed facts and request in one short sentence.
Clean, functional structure: problem statement plus clear action request. It’s not a checklist, but it is organized and easy to act on.
No emotional comfort talk or heavy validation; it simply acknowledges the issue and moves straight to the practical request.
It gives a clear request and a constraint, but still leaves the recipient some room on timing and asks to "confirm the schedule in writing," which is practical rather than overly controlling.
